en.name: FEMALE REPRODUCTIVE SYSTEM
en.info: Inspect the main organs of the female reproductive system in AR and tap each note to read its role.
en.hint: Use rotate, scale and labels while the model is active.

parts.label: Ovary
parts.title: OVARY
parts.info: The ovary stores egg cells and also produces hormones such as estrogen and progesterone.
parts.hint: This small rounded structure sits near the end of the reproductive pathway.

parts.label: Fallopian Tube
parts.title: FALLOPIAN TUBE
parts.info: The fallopian tube carries the egg from the ovary toward the uterus.
parts.hint: This curved passage links the ovary to the uterus.

parts.label: Uterus
parts.title: UTERUS
parts.info: The uterus is the central muscular organ where a fertilized egg can develop during pregnancy.
parts.hint: This is the main chamber at the center of the model.

parts.label: Endometrium
parts.title: ENDOMETRIUM
parts.info: The endometrium is the inner lining of the uterus that thickens and sheds during the menstrual cycle.
parts.hint: This inner tissue layer appears inside the uterus cross-section.

parts.label: Cervix
parts.title: CERVIX
parts.info: The cervix is the lower narrow portion of the uterus that opens into the vaginal canal.
parts.hint: It forms the transition between the uterus and the lower tract.

parts.label: Vagina
parts.title: VAGINA
parts.info: The vagina is the muscular canal that connects the cervix to the outside of the body.
parts.hint: This lower passage continues downward from the cervix.
